en: This requirement is triggered because you indicated your organization maintains valid South African Statutory Labor Insurance documentation in the South Africa Statutory Labor Insurance Applicability Assessment questionnaire.
South Africa Statutory Labor Insurance Certificate

South Africa requires most employers to register with the Compensation Fund, a statutory body managed by the Department of Employment and Labour, to provide insurance for employees against injuries and diseases sustained at work. This requirement is established by the Injuries and Diseases Act (COIDA) of 1993.
\nA Letter of Good Standing (LOGS) is an official document issued by South Africa’s Compensation Fund under the Department of Employment and Labour. It certifies that an employer is up to date and compliant with obligations under the Compensation for Occupational Injuries and Diseases Act (COIDA).
\nCompanies must apply for the LOGS directly from the Compensation Fund, usually through the online portal.
Please upload your company’s valid Letter of Good Standing (LOGS) issued by South Africa's Compensation Fund to confirm compliance with South Africa's Statutory Labor Insurance requirements.
Most Letters of Good Standing are valid for one year from the issue date.
Please provide the expiration date of your company's Letter of Good Standing issued by the Compensation Fund.
